caption: The video is an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the work required per unit mass of gas liquefied. The presentation slide titled "Tutorial - 3" includes a thermodynamic diagram illustrating various processes such as constant temperature (T=const), constant pressure (p=const), and constant enthalpy (h=const). Initially, the slide shows the value of \( \frac{W_c}{\dot{m}} = 326 \) joules per gram. As the video progresses, additional information is introduced, including the efficiency factor \( y = 0.1176 \). Using these values, the calculation for the actual work required per unit mass of gas liquefied (\( \frac{W_c}{\dot{m}_f} \)) is demonstrated, resulting in \( 2772.1 \) joules per gram. The tutorial is presented by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, as indicated at the bottom of the slide.
